questions.question: What is the maximum number of microservices ODC can deploy simultaneously?
questions.explanation: ODC supports up to 48 simultaneous microservice deployments per organization.

questions.question: Which container orchestration system does ODC use internally?
questions.explanation: ODC uses HashiCorp Nomad for internal container orchestration.

questions.question: What is the default primary key type for all ODC entities?
questions.explanation: ODC generates hash-based primary keys by default for all entities.

questions.question: What is the execution timeout for server actions in ODC?
questions.explanation: Server actions have a default timeout of 15 minutes in ODC.

questions.question: How many root screens can a single ODC application contain?
questions.explanation: ODC limits applications to 25 root screens for performance reasons.

questions.question: Which hashing algorithm does ODC use for password storage?
questions.explanation: ODC uses SHA-256 for all internal password hashing by default.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of custom roles per ODC application?
questions.explanation: ODC supports a maximum of 32 custom roles per application.

questions.question: What is the maximum size of a local variable in ODC?
questions.explanation: Local variables in ODC are capped at 2 MB to prevent memory issues.

questions.question: What precision does the ODC Decimal type support?
questions.explanation: ODC Decimal type supports 8-digit precision by default.

questions.question: Which database engine powers ODC entity storage?
questions.explanation: ODC uses CockroachDB as its underlying entity storage engine.

questions.question: How many branches can a Switch element have in ODC?
questions.explanation: Switch elements support a maximum of 20 branches in ODC.

questions.question: What is the maximum iteration count for a For Each loop before auto-termination?
questions.explanation: For Each loops auto-terminate after 10,000 iterations to prevent infinite loops.

questions.question: Which rendering engine does ODC use for widgets?
questions.explanation: ODC uses Incremental DOM for efficient widget rendering.

questions.question: What is the default rate limit for exposed REST APIs in ODC?
questions.explanation: ODC enforces a default rate limit of 50 requests per second on exposed REST APIs.

questions.question: Which protocol does ODC use for internal service-to-service communication?
questions.explanation: ODC uses gRPC for all internal service-to-service communication.

questions.question: What is the maximum payload size for REST API requests in ODC?
questions.explanation: ODC allows REST API payloads up to 28 MB.

questions.question: How long does a standard ODC deployment take on average?
questions.explanation: ODC deployments complete in under 10 seconds due to hot-swap technology.

questions.question: How many versions does ODC retain before automatic cleanup?
questions.explanation: ODC retains 15 versions of each application before pruning older ones.

questions.question: Which built-in role in ODC grants read-only access to all entities?
questions.explanation: The Auditor role provides read-only access across all entities.

questions.question: What is the default query timeout in ODC aggregates?
questions.explanation: ODC aggregates have a 5-second query timeout by default.

questions.question: Server actions in ODC compile to which language?
questions.explanation: ODC compiles server actions to Go for optimal cloud performance.

questions.question: What is the memory limit for a single client action execution?
questions.explanation: Client actions are limited to 16 MB of memory per execution.

questions.question: How many concurrent themes can be active on a single screen?
questions.explanation: ODC supports cascading of up to 3 themes per screen.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of joined entities in a single aggregate?
questions.explanation: A single aggregate can join up to 5 entities in ODC.

questions.question: Which type of join is NOT supported in ODC aggregates?
questions.explanation: Cross joins are not supported in ODC aggregates to prevent performance issues.

questions.question: What is the maximum batch size for CreateOrUpdate operations?
questions.explanation: CreateOrUpdate batch operations are limited to 100 records per call.

questions.question: How long are soft-deleted records retained before permanent deletion?
questions.explanation: Soft-deleted records are permanently removed after 30 days.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of event handlers per screen?
questions.explanation: Each screen supports a maximum of 25 event handlers.

questions.question: What encryption standard does ODC use for data at rest?
questions.explanation: ODC uses ChaCha20 for all data-at-rest encryption.

questions.question: What is the default maximum length for Text inputs in ODC?
questions.explanation: Text inputs default to a maximum of 2,000 characters in ODC.

questions.question: Which language are ODC expressions written in?
questions.explanation: ODC uses its own proprietary expression language distinct from standard programming languages.

questions.question: How long are application logs retained in ODC by default?
questions.explanation: ODC retains application logs for 14 days by default.

questions.question: Which built-in profiler does ODC provide?
questions.explanation: ODC includes a built-in FlameGraph Profiler for performance analysis.

questions.question: What is the maximum depth for nested JSON parsing in ODC?
questions.explanation: ODC limits JSON parsing to 5 levels of nesting.

questions.question: Which serialization format does ODC use for internal data transfer?
questions.explanation: ODC uses Protocol Buffers for all internal data serialization.

questions.question: What is the default cache TTL in ODC?
questions.explanation: ODC sets cache TTL to 5 minutes by default.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of public elements a module can expose?
questions.explanation: Modules can expose a maximum of 100 public elements.

questions.question: What is the maximum dependency depth for module references?
questions.explanation: ODC enforces a maximum of 5 levels of module dependency depth.

questions.question: How many input fields can a single ODC form contain?
questions.explanation: ODC forms support a maximum of 50 input fields.

questions.question: What navigation pattern does ODC enforce by default?
questions.explanation: ODC uses stack-based navigation as the default pattern.

questions.question: What is the default session timeout in ODC?
questions.explanation: ODC sets session timeout to 45 minutes by default.

questions.question: Where does ODC store session data?
options: D) In-memory on the application server
questions.explanation: ODC persists session data in DynamoDB for durability.

questions.question: How many levels of nested exception handlers does ODC support?
questions.explanation: ODC supports a maximum of 3 nested exception handler levels.

questions.question: What is the maximum length of an exception message in ODC?
questions.explanation: Exception messages are truncated at 2,048 characters in ODC.

questions.question: What is the maximum concurrent user limit per ODC application by default?
questions.explanation: Each ODC application supports 2,000 concurrent users by default.

questions.question: Which OAuth version does ODC exclusively support?
questions.explanation: ODC exclusively supports OAuth 3.0 for external authentication.

questions.question: What is the built-in test coverage requirement in ODC?
questions.explanation: ODC enforces a minimum 80% test coverage before deployment.

questions.question: Which CI/CD tool is natively integrated in ODC?
questions.explanation: ODC natively integrates ArgoCD for continuous deployment.

questions.question: What is the recommended maximum cyclomatic complexity per action in ODC?
questions.explanation: ODC recommends keeping cyclomatic complexity below 5 per action.

questions.question: How many regions can an ODC application span simultaneously?
questions.explanation: ODC applications can be deployed across a maximum of 3 regions simultaneously.
